reviews.review_description:
I mean check out the awesome Game Trailer for it!
Why is this Game not being Pushed by Meta!? Why are they Hiding it!
This Game is Absolutely remarkable, A one of a kind on the Quest 3, there is no other game like it on the Q3! 
One guy has made this game, and it beats so many of the top title games... it's a serious contender and I believe everyone should have this in there collection.
Maybe you ask why none of the Bigger YouTubers have touched it... maybe because they don't know about it, or they haven't given it a fair chance, it's not a game for the faint hearted... you gotta invest, but once you do... you'll be addicted and you to will also wonder why Meta have not been giving this game the coverage it very much deserves, and still its getting awesome updates.

You can check out my Review on Youtube if you search Gotcha VR.

Don't miss out on this Hidden Gem, it's Meta's most Kept Secret.

I took a punt on this as I had seen some good reviews and I was not dissapointed. The graphics are perfectly suited to the size of the game map, there are no loading pauses, it feels like the whole map is available, and it's abosultly huge. There is no hand holding in this game, you have to figure things out for yourself, and the progression in gaining skills, equipment and stamina is very well judged. The sense of scale with the landscape, buildings and giant robots is impressive and very immersive. If you have a fear of heights, it may trigger it. The bows and arrow mechanics are very good, the grappling/swinging takes a little practice and is handy for getting out of a bad situation quickly. I love the free form exploration and you can play the game in whatever style you want, from kill everything to stealthily explore on your trusty horse.  Some of the giant battles are overwheming at first, and you will die, a lot, but as your skills and stamina increse, so do your odds of winning. My only real criticisim is that every time you die, you end up at the start location, and until you uncover portals, it's frustrating to have to travel back to where you were. If there was a way to trigger a save point to avoid 10 minutes of travel back to where you died, only to die agian and spend another 10 minutes of travelling. Overall, one of the best games I have played on the quest, and it's so refreshing to have such a great release from an indy developer. Keep up the great works and i'm looking forward to future updates.

From the start its obvious this game doesn't hold your hand, doesn't tell you what to do every minute, or treat you like a child. It's up to you to figure out this expansive world and how to navigate it. Something I welcome

Exploration, discovery, grappling, flying, and brutally hard combat are on full display in this massive open world.  Akin to Zelda, Shadow of the Colossus and Titan Isles, this game was made with love by a fantastic solo developer.  A developer that listens to the community and is constantly accepting feedback to fine tuning this game.  We need more games and developers like this.  VR needs more like this

Support indie/solo devs and buy this great game now!

reviews.review_title: Give it time, it will hook you.
reviews.review_description: If you like to chill out and explore a new world this game will hook you. The more you upgrade your stamina in game the more awesome you feel like swinging around freely. Love the verticallity swinging circles around a huge tower and the. T-pose gliding off it is awesome. The Giants themselves are tough as hell to beat but offer combat that's very engaging and will have you wanting to go back and give it another shot again and again.

Among Giants isn’t just a game—it’s a VR gem. If you’ve ever dreamed of stepping into a world as vast and alive as Breath of the Wild, but with the epic scale of Shadow of the Colossus, this is your moment. Crafted by a solo developer with relentless passion, this is the kind of ambitious, boundary-pushing experience VR was made for.
The open world is a masterpiece—biomes teeming with secrets, towering giants, and mysteries waiting to be uncovered. Whether you’re grappling across chasms, gliding through the skies, or riding your horse into the unknown, every mechanic feels purposeful and thrilling. Combat can be punishingly difficult, but the freedom to explore at your own pace (or avoid fights entirely) keeps the experience deeply personal.
Puzzles unlock hidden structures, robots roam from tiny to titanic, and the sheer sense of scale will leave you breathless. This isn’t just a game; it’s a love letter to VR, a testament to what one developer can achieve, and a must-play for anyone who craves adventure.
Support this. Play this. Live it.

I almost gave up after 20 minutes because the game just throws you in without any explanation and it was frustrating not knowing where to go, what to do, or even how to do things. Then I found that 1: I had grappling hooks on my hands; 2: the annoying voice to follow the star can be turned off and it’s just pointing to where the giants to kill is. With this info things started to be fun and now I can’t wait to have some free time to play more.

It’s a rough game but it’s a lot of fun when you understand its mechanics.

reviews.review_title: The biggest standalone world to explore
reviews.review_description:
If you like open world games, fighting robots, discovering mysteries, swinging like spider man and being amazed by VR scale when you actually meet one of the giants, then this game is a must buy title for you.

Dont be fooled by simplistics graphics. It is super imersive when you are actually in the game. The world is HUGE and there are no loading screens so it is in fact open world game.

There is no handholding in the game, so the beginning can be little bit difficult, but you will learn everything by not giving up and its really rewarding. Be ready for the challenge!

The single developer is very active on discord and the community itself is helpful.

I wanted to rate the game when i finish it but I played already 10 hours and the end is still far XD.

It’s more than just an open world game. It’s an unfolding of discoveries. It’s frustrating at times but so so rewarding as you progress. 

Patience, I’m finding, is key.

So many nooks and crannies to explore. It’s insane.

I typically dont write reviews but this one really is a hidden gem.  Featuring a vast, open world, massive boss fights, a great story and a beautiful sound track, among giants is easily one of the best experiences currently available.

I had been waiting on something like this to drop since I got into vr and while many have tried to recreate something as awe inspiring as shadow of the colossus, none have really been able to capture that vibe the way among giants does.

It was so easy to get completely immersed in this vibrant new world, swinging, gliding, and riding on horse back to each new location, searching for answers and slaying colossi along the way.

Furthermore, recent updates and quality of life enhancements have made this already incredible experience even better.  If you're looking an adventure I highly recommend you look no further than among giants.

reviews.review_title: VR needs more worlds like this
reviews.review_description:
I took a chance on this one based on the trailer and it far exceeded my expectations.  You can spend hours exploring the vast open world, battling giant robots and soaking up the environmental storytelling.  The game rewards you for searching every part of the map with item chests, hidden targets, lore messages, stamina upgrades, and more.  Towers and mountains that reach thousands of feet into the sky can all be scaled with some practice.

If you can look past the ultra-low-poly art style, which certainly makes it easier for the Quest to handle the huge map with no loading screens, the game plays to VR's other strengths.  The movement mechanics (grappling hooks, riding horseback, and cape gliding) are each enjoyable in their own right, the subtle haptics for each weapon and method of traversal do a great job of immersing you in the world, and the soundtrack and environmental audio and set the mood for exploring a mysterious decaying world full of broken robots.

When you begin the game, it drops you into the world with little explanation of where you are, what to do, or even how to jump or use your weapons.  If you've played any VR game with a bow & arrow over your shoulder you should pick everything up right away, so I appreciated that it didn't just throw up diagrams of controllers with labels on every button.

As for the negatives, a couple of times the game ran out of memory and crashed after an hour or two, but I never lost any progress because of it.  Your floating robot companion speaks up every time there's an pickup nearby or you take out an enemy so he ends up talking too often, though you at least have the option to mute him.  The game also recently added a drone ship that will kill you very quickly if you don't find cover, which made it hard to explore at my own pace.  Despite these drawbacks, I still strongly recommend this game if you prefer open-world exploration over linear corridors filled with endless shallow combat.

developer_response.body:
Thank you ❤️ 

The Drone Hunter feedback is much appreciated. I would love to talk to you in detail at my discord: https://discord.gg/2zKzkkEv6g

reviews.review_description: A real gem of a game. Prepare to loose yourself in a big immersive world with a nice atmosphere. It's nothing but brilliant and a shining light of hope - especially after all the news of AAA studios closing down or turning their backs on VR lately.

reviews.review_title: VR was made for such titles!!
reviews.review_description: The dream comes true for all Colossus Shadow lovers. The scale and the design of the VR world is second to none. This was and is my most awaited VR title premiere of this year. Hope it gets the recognition it deserves. Recommend it to anyone that loves the VR as a medium of experiencing something special!

reviews.review_title: VR Veteran playing here, it's awesome
reviews.review_description: I'm a cardboard / Rift VR / Q1/2/3 user, I've been using VR for over a decade and this is rather impressive stuff.  The scale of the giants is absolutely remarkable and the gameplay is engaging.  It's an overlooked gem.

reviews.review_title: Journey in the Shadow of the Colossus
reviews.review_description: If you value exploration and a sense of discovery/wonder in games you're going to absolutely love this title. You can go wherever you want, explore at your own pace - solve puzzles, fight bosses or just soak in the atmosphere. It's impossible to describe just how magical it feels to be in this world and discover its secrets. For fans of games like Journey or Shadow of the Colossus this is a VR dream come true. The graphics are low-poly but beautiful and the views are unbelievable - I don't know how it's possible to render view distance like this on a standalone headset. Music is outstanding. And this game has one of the best title screens and title drops in all of gaming. I just can't recommend this game enough, no words can do it justice. And to the developer - thank you for making this game, artists like you are the reason why I love being a gamer.

reviews.review_title: Rough with some poor design choices
reviews.review_description:
Update:

I've ridden animals lots of times in VR. Most were completely comfortable, one made me slightly uncomfortable after a while. Riding the horse in this game made me feel ill almost immediately.

This game seems jam packed with interesting ideas but between the glitches, occasionally janky controls, and the poor design choices it's pretty much unplayable for me at the moment.

I really, really wanted to love this game but sadly I have to request a refund.

First Impressions:

This game has great potential, but it's too rough to recommend at this point, even on sale.

I've only played through the introduction up to the title screen and even in that short amount of time I ran into numerous issues.

The performance feels a little sluggish.

Controls can be janky and frustrating at times.

One time I glitched through a wall and was rapidly flung through the geometry for a few seconds until the game reset me.

Another time my character started hopping along the ground and rebounding off objects for some unknown reason.

But the worst issues I encountered weren't bugs, they were deliberate choices by the developers that ignore VR best practices and Meta's game certification guidelines. Meta no longer seems to do any certification testing so developers are free to ignore them, but it makes for an uncomfortable experience for the user.

The most egregious design choice is pinning text to one spot on the display like it's a TV screen. Meta, (and anyone who has their facial interface extended to support glasses or uses the Razor interface which is permanently extended), discourage this. I could only read part of the credits on the title screen because they were tucked away in the lower right hand corner of the "TV screen" and half cut off at the edge of the field of view. Even when a game pins text in the center of the screen it's annoying when you naturally move your head to read a wide line of text and the text works against you.

I don't remember if this is in Meta's guidelines, but it's also bad practice in VR to suddenly take control away from the user and move them around as if they were watching a flatscreen game's cutscene. It's totally immersion breaking and it can make VR newbies feel sick.

Before buying this game, I had read reviews both online and on the store app explaining that Among Giants does not hold your hand, so I expected a steep learning curve. However, I didn't realize that this applies to virtually all aspects of the game: exploration (including HOW to explore, which one might think is intuitive) combat, the upgrade system, etc. 

It's not that the game is overly difficult;  it's that it requires a LOT of patience for failure and creative strategy. There are a lot of game features that are not intuitive, but make sense once you figure them out. Even knowing ahead of time that I would need to be patient, I got frustrated with the lack of apparent linear progression, and was ready to give up on it. However, the game gets exponentially easier and clearer as you make that linear progression in small chunks, and then things really open up.

It's a huge, open-world adventure that blends exploration, combat, platforming and puzzles, and it belongs in your library.

It’s been a while since I’ve played a vr game this long, I was up all night playing probably around 6 hours and hardly have any percent complete of the game maybe like 17%, could use a sharper look and  more stability at times but for the most parts it’s worth the price. Must buy hidden gem.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback and kind words! :)

Happy to let you know that I pushed a new patch (1.0.12e) that now sets the default resolution to native 100% after some optimisations

reviews.review_description:
I have been fortunate enough to watch this project evolve over time and have played many iterations. 
It must be stated that this is a solo developer and what a benchmark that has been set. 
Among Giants takes place in a wonderfully designed world with biomes to explore and mysteries to uncover. It's just you, your horse and a droid for company as you encounter great giants around the environment. The game blends the epicness of Shadow of the Collosus and open world exploration such as Breath of the Wild all neatly tied into a VR experience. 
We need more games from developers like this. I know the developer is passionate about this project and I think even those new to his work can see that in Among Giants. 
Play this game and support developers like this, so that the store can filled with equally ambitious games.

reviews.review_title: not for everyone but everyone should try
reviews.review_description: Beautiful grand environment and atmosphere. It's an immersive  narrative game with but you can really go at your leisure and there is a huge amount of exploration. Combat can be punishingly difficult and frustrating but you are somewhat able to control the pace. Some of the movement mechanics are finicky and it seems easy to become unaligned. Still, it's hard not to recommend for nailing an epic desolate feel. It's the kind of game there should be more of in VR.

reviews.review_title: A genuine hidden gem worth playing...
reviews.review_description: The best way to describe the game is kind of like shadow of the collossus with a touch of breath of the wild, but with its own personality...it definitely takes inspiration from those games but still has its own identity...beyond that I'll say the world in the game is very large with no loading screens at all, and while the graphics are on the simpler side for sure it's one of those cases where it may be simple but it all fits together to make it a style rather than a hindrance...there are day night cycles, dynamic weather, and an open world where you can go where you want when you want with puzzles to discover that slowly unlock the lore of the game giving you a sense of purpose to explore if you want to, but are never forced to...and while this is a complete game that can be played and beaten from start to finish, the developer is still putting out quality of life updates as well as listening to the player base and making adjustments based on feedback...I've got over 8 hours in the game and am no where near done unlocking all the lore and haven't beaten it yet...this game doesn't hold your hand but instead lets you explore and learn at your own pace that feels rewarding when you figure things out...I can safely say I highly recommend it in general but especially to fans of games like shadow of the collossus...

So I am 6 hours in and am having a blast exploring the huge open world of this game.   I haven't really tried to complete anything in particular, it's just so fun to explore.  There are several ways to explore. You can walk, go on horse back, glide from heights, and  swing around with dual grappling hooks.    They are all fun!  You have a bow and arrow to take out enemies. There are also small puzzles to get more of the lore of the game.   I like how relaxing it is and that dying isn't punishing at all. You just start back at the spawn point.   All in all just a fantastic experience and amazing from a solo dev.   Highly recommended!
Just dropping some love for this game. We don't get a lot of well crafted open map games like this on standalone. This one definitely deserves the praise.

Among Giants is a fantastic VR open-world game on Quest that mixes the epic scale of Shadow of the Colossus with Zelda-style exploration and discovery.
You get dropped into this huge, beautiful world full of towering giants. No hand-holding — you just wander, climb massive structures, solve clever puzzles, and figure everything out yourself. That “aha!” moment when you finally crack a giant’s weakness feels so rewarding.
The sense of scale in VR is awesome, and you can really tell the solo dev put a ton of love and time into it. The environments look great and movement feels smooth.
It’s a smaller indie game, but it nails what it sets out to do. For me, it’s an easy 5 stars. If you love thoughtful exploration in VR, definitely check it out and get lost among the giants!

Absolutely amazing game, open world exploration in a vr game is something I’ve long looked forward to, robots from small to giant roam the lands giving a good range of combatants to face. The many different abilities from grappling to basically skydiving in a glide suit is awesome, and I love the puzzles you have to solve for unlocking structures. Riding the horse and using the reigns is really fun and the summoning horn is top tier. Also has high replay-ability as well!

Just started it, but already falling in love. I'm a huge fan of games like Tunic, Zelda, Shadow of the Colossus, and if you are too, I think you'll really enjoy this game. Be weary though--it doesn't hold your hand and it's not like every other Ubisoft game that tells you exactly where to go next. But that's really half the fun of these games, right?

Instead this is a game about exploration, discovery, and carving your own path. And it is huge! I've only played an hour or two, but when looking at the base camp map it looks like I've only discovered 5-10% of the world! 

There are a few issues that I'm sure the developer will address, but what's even more surprising is that this is made by a solo developer--K Monkey, which is just crazy. So I gotta cut him so slack.

If you're prone to motion sickness, make sure to put on the blinder and know that there are some occasional physics glitches that might throw you madly into the air. And if that annoying floating sphere bumps into my head one more time--!!! (Seriously, please give us an update where we can disable the floating guy temporarily.) 

But really, even with these minor issues, the sense of discovery and scale is unmatched in any other VR game I tried. Where else in VR can you ride around an ancient land on your trusty stead? Or glide through the air using a wingsuit? Or traverse a digital landscape as big as New York City!?! No where. That's where. 

So go buy this game now and support indie developers!

reviews.review_title: A game with discovery at its core
reviews.review_description:
Inspired by games like Shadows of the Colossus and Breath of the Wild, Among Giants is an open world VR game that doesn't hold your hand and rewards exploration and experimentation.

You, as the hero, are plopped down into this world with no standard tutorial to speak of. You'll quickly discover that Among Giants expects the player to figure out where to go, what to do and how to do it, mostly on your own.

The whole learning process is user driven. The fact that there was no standard tutorial, threw me off at first. It's been a long time since a game made it my job to learn its mechanics and the rules of its world on my own. But I quickly adapted and was reminded how rewarding it is to discover things on your own through organic exploration and trial and error.

Your companions on this journey are a small floating robot that will highlight points of interest and heal you in battle as well as a horse that you can call with a horn. With the two of them to help you, the huge open world of Among Giants is yours to explore.

Riding the horse to ride involves simple mechanics that feel intuitive once you figure them out. Push the reins forward or back to control your speed. You can even take your hands off the reins, and your horse will continue straight ahead while you fire your arrows like you're a Mongolian horse archer. Other weapons that can be found include grenades and firearms including laser pistols and rifles and grenade launchers.

The game includes wrist mounted grapple mechanics that allow great skill expression by trying to chain swings together like Spiderman, to quickly traverse terrain or get to a higher elevation.

There's also a gliding mechanic that is as simple as stretching out your arms to either side of you. This will slow your descent through the air and allow you to traverse and also scout out the world from a bird's eye view.

There's a great sense of scale when soaring high above everything, or when you're on the ground fighting Giant robots that tower over you.

There are also a couple translation mini games to help you decrypt the text of this world. At first I wasn't sure if I was supposed to be able to read the words that occasionally popped up on my screen but then I discovered that when you breach into enemy bases, you first have to do a Wordle-eque translation mini game with different symbols. Each time you complete a puzzle, more of the symbols will be converted into English text, allowing you to understand the world around you even more.

The graphical style is very minimal, but still distinct and beautiful in its own right. Music and sound effects are top notch, but the mixing on some sound effects is a bit loud and seems to be localized to the right ear.

A small issue was clipping through a few walls, getting stuck inside terrain or flinging myself into the stratosphere with my grappling hooks.

However these issues were few and far between and the developer, K Monkey, is receptive to feedback and is still implementing changes and quality of life upgrades.

I've still got plenty more to play, but for now I'm confident in giving Among Giants a huge thumbs up.

The opening scene, is one to behold, AMONG GIANTS in front of you is a stand out feature, and then the music kicks in and wow, you feel at peace with the world.
No real idea what my goal is, but exploration is the name of the game, my little flying robot is guiding me and I just feel standing on the cliff edge looking out across the vast land.
A little tutorial would be really helpful for anyone new to this kind of game.
But it’s a wonderful experience that people need to have in their collection.

reviews.review_title: A Beautiful and Immersive VR Experience
reviews.review_description: is a really unique and immersive experience on Meta Quest that stands out from typical VR games. The environments are beautiful and thoughtfully designed, The sound design also adds a lot to the immersion. Overall, definitely worth checking out if you enjoy immersive, slower-paced VR experiences. Looking forward to seeing how it evolves!

I never heard of the game until yesterday and man was I blown away! It reminded me a lot of the new Zelda parts on the Switch but the dev did an awesome job at porting that "just explore at that pace what you want" feeling in VR. It looks gorgeous and you feel the love for the game on all the small details. What a great game 😮 Mechanics feel great and it offers the opportunity to discover a lot of stuff everywhere, it feels rewarding to mess around.

 For me honestly a huge surprise and a living proof that VR is not dead but very well alive.

The best open world experience in VR

, unafraid not to hold your hand! 

Shadow of the Collosus-esque scale meets sweeping open world discovery. The sort of game you don't want to read too much about, so I don't want to spoil too many of the features, just boot it up and experience it for yourself! While a little rough around the edges in some areas, its understandable given the grand ambition of the title from a solo dev.

For those like me who enjoy a challenge and the feeling of figuring out things for themselves, I highly recommend! Its intriguing indie gems like this game that folks on this store should be helping support in my book!

This is quite ambitious and surprisingly immersive VR adventure that really leans into the sense of scale that only virtual reality can deliver. From the moment you step into its world, the freedom to explore.  It’s large, open, and encourages you to explore at your own pace.  I personally felt lost and really struggled with what ‘and how’ to do next.  So for me I feel a tutorial option would have helped but I expect others will say that’s the joy of the game. 
I loved the graphic style and the sound, I have only played for a short time as I do suffer VR motion sickness and boy I certainly did in this game.  I did find the settings to help me, it did marginally but for me I will be playing in short bursts, so bare that in mind if you do suffer it is a moderate comfort level game..
There’s also a nice sense of atmosphere. The minimalist storytelling and environmental clues work well if you enjoy piecing things together yourself, rather than being led by the hand.
However, it’s not without its flaws. At times, the world can feel a bit empty between key moments, and the lack of clear direction may leave some players wandering without purpose. 
It doesn’t quite reach perfection, but it’s absolutely worth your time—especially if you enjoy exploration, scale, and a bit of mystery in your VR games.

I’d just like to say well done so far, at first I thought (just another cash grab with some lazy dev work), then I played it…. I don’t know if you are only one person or a couple of people but I was impressed by what has been accomplished SO FAR this feels like a little bit more than a shadow of the colossus clone this has its own atmosphere and not just titanic machines to take out but enemies of around normal (ish) size, I played just over an hour and havnt even taken Out the first machine yet I’m impressed, impressed by what this world looks like if you can appreciate the engines visual yes their are a few visual glitches and a few times where the frame rate went a bit funky but with time I’m hoping this will be ironed out, I hope for more quality of life at the minimum but hope you get the attention you so deserve, I will be posting the full game on my YT channel in the future, keep going.

This is a big world with some cool ideas, and it's a huge endeavor for a solo developer. However...

​The interactions are clumsy and often unintuitive. You often find yourself wondering how to reproduce an action you just performed, or whether a frustrating moment was an intentional design choice or simply a lack of clarity. Optimization is lacking; the FPS is poor to the point of motion sickness, and horseback riding doesn't feel good. I constantly found myself staring at the horse's back when I try to mount it.

​The color palette is dull; plain colors produce gradients that look like graphical artifacts rather than a stylistic choice. While the world is large, it is filled with "boring emptiness," especially inside the buildings I explored.

Some UI choices don't look like they were tested in VR. I was forced to stare at a puzzle screen for 15 minutes with my head tilted back, as if I were trying to find a fly on the ceiling. It was incredibly uncomfortable.

There are almost none of the settings you would expect to find in a VR game. For example, there is no option to change your dominant hand.

​I would play this if it had 1/10th of the ambition but 10 times the polish. Right now, it is a collection of underbaked ideas that leave the experience lacking.
​If you want guided, fun exploration with verticality and beautiful landscapes within a limited color palette, check out Into Black. Want to see riding implemented without causing motion sickness? Look at Asgard's Wrath 2. For fun parkour, try Reach.

Has the same style of ambience as Jet Island and the Team Ico games. I like that it pops you in and makes you think for yourself. Gives a nice feeling of discovery. The world is intriguing and has a sort of bitter sweet feeling.

reviews.review_title: Zelda vibes but needs some work
reviews.review_description: First off, riding a horse, is fricken awesome! And the views are awesome. There’s a ginormous flying whale! Music is a vibe. But hardly seen any “giant” robots so far… Oh and there’s random fun puzzles throughout the area. the most frustrating thing about the game so far is the companion bot. His battery life is really low. After a full recharge, he was low battery and kept bugging me to recharge him no much later. It also telling me to do tasks that I’ve already done or doesn’t make any sense. For example, it would point at a pile of rocks with an explosion sign. There’s a explosive barrel nearby it so of course I’d assume it’s telling me to explode to hopefully discover something. NOPE. Just nearly get myself killed and a boat load of nothing.  Additionally, when at its recharge station, it would keep pointing back at it basically saying it needs a charge but it’s already charged fully. So I literally have to drag it away for it to stop demanding to be recharged. it would also highlight the portal thing and tell me to go through it. I did this once and it kept pointing at it, so I did again with no change as it just teleported me to the other side of the portal. I tried one last time while riding the horse, and we got glitched into one of the boulders and I couldn’t get out at all.  It’s also strange there’s no backpack. I had to forgo the bamboo stick to make room for the gun. In an open world concept game, a backpack is absolutely a necessity so strange not to have one here. Unless it’s unlocked or found somewhere I’m not where of. All in all, a magical game that just needs some kinks to be worked out. Still recommend it

developer_response.body:
UPDATE:
Latest version 1.0.2 and above address the companion bot battery life

-------

Firstly, thank you for the feedback :)

I have submitted a new patch for the companion bot battery life. A full charge has been increased from 15 minutes to 1 hour and Energy Cells restore 5 minutes up from 1 minute. Please note though that when it gets hurt, it will chip away at its power too.

I will be implementing more smarter POI detection for the bot too soon.

reviews.review_description: not sure if a bug but my horn won’t activate in the first few minutes of gameplay, character is stuck and no matter the motion i use for the horn, nothing happens. tried restarting and same thing / what am i missing? can’t find a discord either - for a game with zero instructions and no way to find help, it’s pretty bad

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Patch 1.0.11b build 172 has a fix for a potential issue you described here. Thank you for your understanding.
=====
Discord
https://discord.gg/sRUjG7TAkw

Thanks for the message.

You are in the tutorial and the hologram man in front of you is showing how to use the Horn.

You grab the Horn from the left side of your waist and bring it to your mouth like the Hologram is showing. The horn will blow correctly and summon the Horse.

The Horn will blow with just air and no Horn sound if there is not enough space to Summon the Horse so you need to be in an open area.

reviews.review_description: Awesome!

I've spent around 3hrs playing this game, and I can't believe this wasn't advertised more. 
  This is peak VR gaming 👌. It's not easy, it is amazing to ride a horse in vr that you can go anywhere at anytime. The first hr I just rode around absolutely immersed. If you like Zelda you'll love this game. Meta should consider putting the dev on payroll, leave him alone and let him cook!

I played for over an hour and was completely immersed in exploring the world. I really like that the game doesn't hold your hand and instead encourages you to discover things on your own.

Finding new weapons was enjoyable, the grapple hook is fun (though a little clunky to use at times), and the gliding feels fantastic.

One feature I'd love to see added is a comfort option for horseback riding. When turning on the horse, the camera rotates automatically, which makes me feel a bit queasy. In Asgard's Wrath 2, the camera didn't move for you while riding, and I found that to be a much more comfortable experience.

Overall, this game shows a lot of promise, and I'm excited to jump back in and continue exploring the world.

A genuinely open-world experience on Quest, comparable to Compass, Revria, or Asgard’s Wrath 2. Comparisons to Zelda: Breath of the Wild feel a bit unfair and tend to raise expectations this game doesn’t aim to meet. Still, its sense of exploration, scale, and atmosphere are superb. It’s not a graphical showpiece, but Im the kind of player that prefers good gameplay over cutting edge visuals — and there’s a huge amount of content here to dive into. I’ll keep returning to this challenging, thoughtful,  and excellent gem. My only two minor gripes: the eagle sound repeats a bit too often, and there’s an occasional screen-door effect.

Recommended! Buy it!

How DARE meta store push sloppy, free games at me as new releases and then hide this gem, NAY, near masterpiece of a game from me! No coming soon or nothing?! This game is down right freaking amazing! I am CRAVING it after playing for 2 rounds of almost 4 hours a piece yesterday lol SO much to do and so big and even though I am 'flying' around and grappling all over the place, there is no motion sickness experienced! Reminds me very much of Zelda Ocarina of tine, and that is what I look for in an adventure game! You even get a daggum horse!!!! I forget all about him cause I am constantly flying around but yeah, you can even ride a horse.

Now there are some things to be fixed but nothing major or that any other vr game doesn't experience when first released. Grabbing the shield item to remove it from the generator or whatever it is makes you dash forward over and over again where it is very difficult to grab it. I wish I could use my robot to scan things to know exactly what they are, like a brief description. I also clipped through a wall in the big satellite building right by the beginning 2 times, 1 of which sent me FLYING into the sky making a reload necessary. Also, a back back would be great! Plus maybe some funny Lil npcs to flush out the environments!

Other than that, great job and even if you don't add or fix any of the above list, I still give you 5 stars and a round of applause!!! Thank you my friend.

This game has a great potential and I hope you will had multiplayeurs soon ! 
There are still some bugs (I was stuck inside a wall of a building and I had to restart the game once). 
Please continue to develop it !

reviews.review_title: I don't normally give a perfect score...
reviews.review_description:
I've played Among Giants for around 10 hours now and I have only reached the half way point (I think, NO SPOILERS!). I have played enough now to write an honest review and I have to give this a perfect score.

In the 10 hours played, I have explored a mysterious world where there are secrets around every corner. It is huge. It is hard to describe just how BIG this game is. To travel across the landscape, I can use my Horse, fly/glide or use my grapple to swing around. All these options give me, the player, so many fun ways to explore this world.

Across my travels, I have had to battle across a range of different robots but the main attraction are the Giants. These giants roam the land one at a time and is similar to hunting down monsters in Monster Hunter. I have seen walking giants, massive spider queens and also a serpent dragon thing. These battles with the giants need patience and skill but when you take one down, it will respawn you like in Shadow of the Colossus.

There are also the hacking elements all over the game. When you begin, most of the game is unreadable in some robot alien language. This is like in Tunic. As you play the game more, the text becomes more clear and readable, slowly revealing the mystery to the player. It's quite a wonderful idea and each time i come back to the game, the text at the start becomes more teasing as i begin to put together the story.

Lastly, I need to talk about the music and audio. This is near perfect, I cannot fault this. As you travel across the game, you will be listening to the atmosphere of the world as well as a beautiful musical score. I have almost cried when reaching the peak of a massive tower and the music swells just in time.

Review score: 5 out of 5 starts PERFECT. Best game of the year for me. Well done developer, well done.
